the-circle-4-intersection-of-line-and-ci.question: Q2(b)
the-circle-4-intersection-of-line-and-ci.note: **tangent condition** — a line is tangent to a circle ⟺ the **perpendicular distance from the centre to the line equals the radius**. Set up the equation, square both sides, solve the resulting quadratic

the-circle-9-simultaneous-equations.question: Q4(b)
the-circle-9-simultaneous-equations.note: find the equation of a circle given **three points on it** — exploit the geometric observation that two of the points share an x-coordinate, so the chord between them is **vertical**, which pins down the y-coordinate of the centre

the-circle-2-characteristics-of-the-equa.question: Q2(a)
the-circle-2-characteristics-of-the-equa.note: **circle equation from centre + one point** — radius equals the distance from the centre to the given point; then plug into the standard circle equation `(x − h)² + (y − k)² = r²` (page 19 of the log tables)

the-circle-2-characteristics-of-the-equa.question: Q2(b)
the-circle-2-characteristics-of-the-equa.note: **symmetries of a circle** — any point obtained by reflecting a known point through the centre (or across the horizontal/vertical diameter through the centre) is also on the circle
